copy.tagline: A 90-unit workforce housing community in Greensboro's growth corridor — acquired at a discount to replacement cost and positioned for meaningful NOI expansion through a targeted renovation program and operational repositioning.
copy.thesis: Four Seasons Townhomes is a cash-flowing, 90-unit townhome community in one of the Piedmont Triad's most resilient submarkets. At acquisition, the property carried below-market rents and deferred maintenance that suppressed NOI — precisely the conditions that create a value-add opportunity. Our renovation program upgrades unit interiors and common areas over the first 12–18 months, driving rents to market levels and pushing stabilized NOI toward $925K+ by Year 2. Agency debt at a fixed 5.23% IO rate provides predictable carry while the renovation program executes. With a 7-year hold and a projected 6.25% exit cap against a stabilized NOI above $1.1M, the deal underwrites to a 3.2× equity multiple for LPs. The asset is co-GP'd by Ginkgo Residential, an experienced regional operator with a proven renovation track record in the Triad market.
copy.marketNotes: The Piedmont Triad — Greensboro, Winston-Salem, High Point — has consistently attracted corporate relocations and light manufacturing expansion, driving household formation and sustained multifamily demand. Vacancy in the submarket has remained below 5% for six consecutive quarters. New supply is limited by construction costs that now exceed $180K per door, creating a durable rent floor for workforce housing. Greensboro's median household income supports rents at our target levels with acceptable rent-to-income ratios, and demand from UNCG and surrounding employers anchors occupancy through economic cycles.

marketFindings.headline: Value-Add Properties Lead Rent Growth
marketFindings.body: Greensboro value-add multifamily posted +5.4% YoY rent growth in 2025 — the strongest segment in the metro. Class-A rents are flat while Class-C and workforce repositioning assets command the largest rent spreads, validating the renovation-driven underwriting.

marketFindings.headline: Supply Pipeline Constrained at 4.7% of Stock
marketFindings.body: The Piedmont Triad's active development pipeline of ~4,600 units represents only 4.7% of the existing 98,000-unit base. With replacement costs exceeding $180K per door, new workforce-tier townhome construction is economically infeasible — creating a structural rent floor for existing assets.
marketFindings.source: Kirkland Co. Piedmont Triad Market Report; NCHousing data

marketFindings.headline: Diversified Employment Anchors Rental Demand
marketFindings.body: Toyota's $13.9B EV battery plant (Liberty, NC — 40 min from Greensboro), Cone Health (18,000+ employees), Honda Aircraft, and Volvo represent a diversified employer base driving sustained household formation. NC faces a projected 760,000-unit housing shortage by 2029, keeping absorption strong.
marketFindings.source: NC Dept. of Commerce; HUD PD&R; NC Housing Coalition

marketFindings.headline: Metro Vacancy at 7.8% — Elevated vs. History
marketFindings.body: HUD reported 7.8% apartment vacancy in the Greensboro–High Point MSA (Q4 2024), above the 5–7% stabilized range. Elevated vacancy is concentrated in Class-A downtown deliveries; workforce 2BR/1.5BA townhomes — like Four Seasons — historically operate well below metro average, as reflected in the property's current 100% occupancy.
marketFindings.source: HUD PD&R Housing Market Profile, Nov 2024
